summary:
INTIMACY WITH GOD: Brandon shares a message about developing a deep, personal friendship with God, drawing a parallel between spiritual devotion and the daily commitment required in a marriage. He recounts a moment when the Lord revealed He no longer knew a specific individual, prompting Brandon to reflect on how true intimacy is built over time rather than overnight. Brandon contrasts a shallow religious routine — such as attending weekly church services and praying briefly only to present a list of demands — with a mature, two-way relationship. He emphasizes that spiritual maturity involves seeking God's agenda rather than personal desires, and he notes that going through life's trials strengthens this spiritual bond.

SPIRITUAL PRACTICES AND BIBLICAL EXAMPLES: Brandon details his approach to deepening his spiritual connection, which includes meditating on scripture, listening to worship music, and worshiping along with recordings of Kim Clement singing about the mountain of the Lord. He explains that seeking God without an agenda is the pathway to experiencing spiritual vision and angelic encounters. To illustrate his points, Brandon references biblical figures, noting how God shared hidden plans with Abraham regarding Sodom and Gomorrah because of their friendship, and how King David maintained profound intimacy with the Father despite being a fierce warrior. He also warns of the biblical passage where Jesus tells those who performed miracles in His name to depart because He never truly knew them.

INTERCESSORY PRAYER FOR HEALTH AND NATION: Brandon offers an extensive prayer covering both personal and national concerns. He asks for physical healing, specifically mentioning thyroid conditions, cataracts, ear issues, arthritis, and colon cancer, alongside requests for financial blessings, better jobs, and the restoration of marriages. On a broader scale, Brandon prays for the protection of truck drivers, medical personnel, law enforcement officers, and the Department of Homeland Security. He specifically asks for the security of the nation's borders to prevent harm and child trafficking, concluding with a plea for national blessing and protection.
rawData: [January 17, 2024]-[Are you a friend of God]-[https://youtu.be/pNmMm0iQvJw]-[Hey, good morning family. I have another video for you all today. I want to talk to you about being the friend of God and more so what that means to me, but what I feel in my heart that you all need to know. I pray for people, like I told you last video, whenever I talk to people on the phone, the Lord will reveal things to me about them. It was interesting because I was asking the Lord about an individual and He said, "Brandon, I don't know them. I used to know them, but I don't know them anymore." That took me back and I said, "Well, Lord, what do you mean by that?" He said, "Brandon, how do you know somebody? How do you get to know somebody?" I said, "Well, Lord, you spend time with them. You're intimate with them." He said, "Do the people that watch your videos—you've made over 100 videos—do you feel like they know you?" I said, "No, Lord, they don't know me at all. They may know a lot about me, my history, and things that You've done for me, but do they truly know you?" I said, "No, not at all." They don't know the intimacy that my wife and I have. How is that developed? It is developed day by day in intimacy and a friendship, just like a marriage is developed day by day. If you hear any noises, it's my daughter again, Megan; she's sitting in here watching VeggieTales. Intimacy is developed day by day; it is never an overnight thing. Like I said, you met your husband or your wife and you had a courtship. You were so excited to go on these dates and the relationship was fresh; you had those puppy dog eyes and you were excited to go out to the movies or go out to dinner. You have that relationship, that courtship, and you're excited about that relationship, but when you get married, there's an intimacy that develops beyond a boyfriend and girlfriend, beyond anything you can comprehend. Of course, if you've been married for a while, you know—I've been married 23 years—so you start to really know that individual: their likes, their facial expressions, the smells, everything. You know that individual, but it is developed over time; it is not developed in a day. So a lot of people think they go to church on a Sunday, and that's what the Lord shows me. They go to church on a Sunday, they may volunteer maybe once a month, and they have an hour service. They go to that hour service, they may pray for ten minutes a day, and they may read their Bible for five minutes a day. But just think about this: imagine if you did that exact same thing with your spouse or your loved one and you only talked to them five minutes a day. Your main conversation was about an hour on Sundays when you go to church; after that, you have some fellowship at dinner, and then you don't talk to them anymore but maybe five or ten minutes a day from then on—never on the phone, nothing—and you're doing all the talking. You're rehearsing everything that you want from them: "I need this and this and this. God, I thank you that you healed Johnny and you're healing Susie, and you're going after this one who is lost. Lord, I have all these needs, and I'm not feeling well in my body today. Lord, I have all this stuff going on at work; my boss is a real jerk and I've got this deadline. Could you help me with some money?" Then you turn around and say, "Amen," and then you're gone. He doesn't hear from you anymore that day other than when you need something again. Every single time you go to the Lord, you need something again. You don't really ever hear His heart. Prayer is a two-way conversation; He's going to talk to you and you're going to talk to Him. But most people think prayer is just baby steps. This is like little children stuff: "Father, I need, I need, I need; I have wants and desires. Bye!" But if you want to grow up in the relationship and you want to come up to a higher place with Him... people say, "How do you see in the spirit? How do you have visitations from angels? How have you had eight visitations from Jesus? How have you seen into the throne room like that? I want you to help me to get there because I don't know how to get there. I don't hear God." I read so many comments in the comment section from people asking me how to hear God. It is developed. It says, "Those that seek me will find me; those that knock, I'll open the door unto him." Relationship is developed day to day, all day long. The Bible says to pray without ceasing. Seeking Him and finding Him automatically tells me that God is hiding in some way. It's like hide and seek. "God, where are you? I want to be intimate with you. I want to go to another level. What's on your heart?" People ask if I pray a certain way to get Him to show me things. I want to say it like this: not one time do I ever pray with the intentions of seeking and seeing. If I pray, I'm praying because I'm intimate. I want to touch His garment. I want what's on His heart. "Lord, what's on your heart today? Let me help you. What is grieving you today? What's your agenda, not my agenda?" I don't pray for Him to touch my agenda; I pray for His agenda for me, mirroring it back and saying, "God, not my will but Yours be done." How can I come up higher in my thinking? The Bible says that you have the mind of Christ. You can think like Christ thinks by meditating on His word, getting scripture inside of you. I try very seldom to—and I'm just being real with y'all—I get in the flesh too and I go to him and say, "Lord, I have needs. I need my puppies to sell. I need all this stuff." But He said He knows you have need of things before you even ask. Jesus said, "Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come, thy will be done." He knows everything you need before you even ask it. So I go to Him not insulting Him, but thanking Him for it. "Thank you, Father, for healing for my family. No colds, no viruses, no nothing come nigh. I thank you, Father, that your word says that by your stripes we are healed. I thank you, Father, that you said in your word that those that seek you will find you. Now, Father, whatever is on your heart today, I thank you that I pray the mysteries and the secret things. And if you have anything to show me today, Lord, not in the flesh, I thank you that I see what I'm supposed to see and I hear what I'm supposed to hear from You, that You said I will hear Your voice and a stranger's voice I'll not follow. Now, Satan, I bind you in the name of Jesus and I command you to bow your knee. You get out of here, you leave me alone. I plead the blood over my prayer time and I plead the blood over me that I hear the Lord's voice and I'm not going to be tormented by the tormentor." I go to Him with worship. What really blesses me is Kim Clement. He has worship things on YouTube and I listen to him worshiping on "The Mountain of the Lord." I record it on my phone and I put it on repeat, just hearing him talk about the mountain of the Lord. I feel the presence of the Lord so strong whenever that man sings, and he went home to be with the Lord years ago. I listen to that and I listen to piano music, like "I Surrender All." You just get in the presence of the Lord. Find what ministers to you and gets you in that river. Think about Lot in Sodom and Gomorrah. Lot was Abraham's nephew. He's in the midst of all the trash going on and doesn't even know that Sodom and Gomorrah is about to be judged and wiped off the face of the Earth. But God comes to Abraham because he's His friend. God will come to you and start speaking to you and showing you the hidden things. There's a lot of things I probably shouldn't say on here, but out of compassion, I try to tell you all what's coming and what to pray about. Some of this stuff people are not ready for. There are some of you that are mature and understand, but there are a lot of babies on here that don't know, so I'm trying to take you to the next level, teaching you about being a friend of God. When the Bible says that Jesus says, "Depart from me, I never knew you," people say, "Lord, I healed the sick in your name and cast out devils in your name." But He says to you at the end, "Depart from me, I never knew you." He never knew you. That means you can have a relationship with somebody and talk to them, but they don't know you. You can have a conversation at church, but you still don't know Him. You probably do not know your pastor; he preaches at you for an hour every Sunday, but you don't know his intimacy behind closed doors. You can get to know the Father in a relationship that deep, where He will come and start sharing the hidden things and the secret things that He won't talk about to anybody else. He'll start sharing intimate things because it was developed out of relationship. You have a different relationship with every child you have. You love them all, but you might not be a friend to all of them. A friendship is developed. It's about who wants to spend more time with me. Does that child sit around and say, "Dad, let's go have a coffee or play outside"? They're always asking to be at your feet. That is a lot different than the one saying, "I'm going to hang out with my friends, I'll see you in a week." I hope this makes sense. Going deeper in the relationship with Him is like a marriage. If you don't spend time with your spouse, you are not going to be intimate. The more time you spend with them, the more you talk to them and understand them. You have hours of conversations because you love them and you have this burning desire to know them. It's time for you to come up higher. People want to know about the intimacy of seeing into the spirit and seeing angels, but it doesn't come with a shallow relationship; it comes with a refiner's fire. A good marriage is always tested. It's when you go through the fire with that person that you put a pull on the chain, and the stronger the chain, the more it will last. When you go through the trials of life and spiritual pressure from the demonic and you're still strong and say, "Father, though this tries to slay me, yet will I praise You. No matter how hard this trial is, I worship You. I keep my eyes on You. I will not be moved by what I see or feel; I'm moved by what the word of God says." Keeping your eyes on Him when you're hurting and broken, and you just keep pushing and being real with Him—when you get real with God, God will get real with you. He knows the secrets and the intents of your heart. When you go to Him in prayer without an agenda, it's like talking to your spouse. If you were talking to your wife and looking at your watch or phone every five minutes, it's boring. But if you lose track of time while worshiping and an hour feels like five minutes, that's love. That's the way it is when you're passionately in love and pursuing them. When He says, "I don't know them," and yet they say they're a Christian, that's a very scary place to be. Does the Father know you, or does He just know a lot about you? Do you casually converse with Him for ten minutes with an agenda of what you need? He loves you and desires intimacy, just like He did with Abraham. David was a man after God's own heart; he was passionately in love with the Lord. He was a warrior—a man's man who killed lions and bears—yet he had such compassion and intimacy with the Father. You read Psalms and see this guy had it; he was quick to repent and always loving on the Father. That was under the Old Covenant; we have a New Covenant with Jesus. Think of how much deeper we can have a relationship and the glory of the Lord can rest on your household. I want to go deeper with the Father in 2024. Where will it be in 2025? Will He start showing you the secret things and reveal to you witty ideas and revelations? Let me pray for you all today. Father, I thank you for every person watching this. I pray a hedge of protection on them, that they would be intimate with You and You would call them a friend. We want to be on fire for Jesus, and our relationship should reflect You. I thank you for taking these people to another level of knowing You, that Your word would become Rhema to their minds, bodies, and spirits. I thank you for every single one of them. For the people who've blessed me, I pray over their finances. I thank you for better jobs, creative ideas to get wealth, bonuses, sales, and growth in business. I call healing and wholeness over their bodies. I speak to thyroids to be healed and whole in Jesus' name. They're delivered from satanic bondage. I call for cataracts of the eyes to be healed and creative miracles in their eyes. I call for physical and spiritual ears to open right now with new hairs and new follicles. I speak to arthritis to go; Christ has redeemed us from the curse of the law. We speak to gallbladders and stomach issues. We curse colon cancer in the name of Jesus and command it to go now. We call for restoration in marriages and relationships with children. We thank you for new houses, new homes, and new cars. I thank you for better-paying jobs for them right now in Jesus' name. We take authority over demonic spirits in the workplace and command them to cease and be still. We plead the blood of Jesus over their work environment. We thank you that Your angels are going before us and protecting us by the blood. We pray for truck drivers, EMTs, hospital workers, and for the police. Show them hidden things from criminals and protect our officers, our sheriffs, and the Department of Homeland Security. We pray for our borders, that You would keep the riff-raff out and the people trying to cause harm or trafficking children. I pray a protection over this nation and that You rise up and call this nation blessed. We thank you that Jesus is coming soon for us. We want intimacy with You and we thank you that we get to spend eternity crying, "Holy, holy, holy is the Lord." Thank you, Lord, for always meeting our needs and taking care of my family. In Jesus' name, amen. Well, I just wanted to thank you all for watching. If this video blessed you, please click that like and subscribe button. You are helping get the gospel throughout the world. Thank you for your support and your love. Put a smile on your face and a song in your heart. Jesus loves you so much and we will see you next time. Bye-bye.]
